data.title: Carter's plays defense with a poison pill to prevent a hostile takeover
data.description:
Carter's (NYSE:CRI) announced on Wednesday that it adopted a temporary stockholder rights plan because one investor unexpectedly bought a large amount of its shares and may be buying more. The plan is aimed at preventing any investor from secretly taking control of the company without properly compensating all shareholders or giving the board time to evaluate deals.

The company will give stockholders a special right attached to each share they own starting October 3. Those rights only become active if someone buys 15% (or 20% for certain investors) of shares without board approval. If triggered, affected shareholders can either swap the right for a share of Carter's (NYSE:CRI) or buy Carter's (NYSE:CRI) at a reduced price, which will make it very costly for anyone trying a hostile takeover.

The buyer of the large stake in Carter's (CRI) is RWWM Inc., which is a California-based registered investment advisor that manages over $1.3 billion in assets.

Shares of Carter's (CRI) were down 0.8% in premarket trading to $29.28 vs. the 52-week range of $23.38 to $68.69. Short interest on Carter's (CRI) stands at 11.6% of the total float.

data.title: Heineken's deal for FICO is expected to boost its LatAm footprint and growth profile
data.description:
Heineken (OTCQX:HEINY) announced earlier in the week that it will acquire 100% of FIFCO's beverage and retail business for $2.3 billion. The transaction continues a partnership from 1986, strengthened in 2002 when a 25% stake in FIFCO’s beverage business in Costa Rica was acquired. The deal is expected to be completed in the first half of 2026.

Of note, FIFCO is the market leader in Costa Rica with 75% volume share in the beer category, per Euromonitor. In Panama, Heineken is the number two player with a 45% share. Heineken (HENIY) management expects the deal to be accretive for margins and EPS after closing.

Weighing in on the deal, HSBC analyst Carlos Laboy said Heineken can improve the operation since per capita consumption of beer in Costa Rica is half of Mexico. "Better revenue management is on the agenda, perhaps by emphasizing more refillable bottle segmentation and strengthening the core-plus and high-end portfolio with Amstel, Mexican brands, Heineken, and craft beers," he highlighted.

Laboy also noted that unlike Heineken's (OTCQX:HEINY) complex and time-consuming deal with Distell and NBL in South Africa in 2023, this deal seems simpler and should be faster to complete. 

HSBC kept a Buy rating on Heineken (OTCQX:HEINY). Laboy and his team like the consumer-centric model and overarching strategy. "With rising concerns of slowing organic volume growth and margin expansion in Europe, this deal strikes us as constructive. Heineken's net debt is expected to increase by EUR3.2bn, implying a c30bp increase in its leverage (net debt/EBITDA) ratio," he wrote. Looking ahead, Heineken (OTCQX:HEINY) is seen as still having enough balance sheet flexibility for further M&A if some of its other minority interests need to be similarly stepped up into control stakes in markets like Chile, Argentina, and Colombia.

data.title: Thor GAAP EPS of $2.36 beats by $1.13, revenue of $2.52B beats by $200M
data.description:
• Thor press release (NYSE:THO): Q4 GAAP EPS of $2.36 beats by $1.13.
  • Revenue of $2.52B (-0.4% Y/Y) beats by $200M.

  • For fiscal 2026, the Company’s full-year financial guidance includes:

    • Consolidated net sales in the range of $9.0 billion to $9.5 billion
    • Stable gross margin at midpoint, with upside in a stronger market
    • Diluted earnings per share in the range of $3.75 to $4.25
    • Guidance assumes a low- to mid-single digit retail decline in North America with stable market share

data.title: ESSA Pharma cuts shareholder payout in revised Xeno deal
data.description:
ESSA Pharma (NASDAQ:EPIX) fell above 18% premarket on Wednesday after the company revised terms of its merger with biotech firm XenoTherapeutics, lowering expected shareholder payout due to potential liabilities and updated cash estimates.

Under the amended agreement, ESSA shareholders are now expected to receive about US$0.12 per common share at closing, in addition to one contingent value right (CVR) per share, and excluding the roughly US$1.69 per share already distributed.

Each CVR gives shareholders the right to receive up to US$0.14 per CVR, for a total potential payout of up to US$6.7 million, depending on certain contingent liabilities.

Previously, shareholders were expected to receive around US$1.91 per share plus a CVR. On August 22, 2025, ESSA distributed approximately US$1.69 per share as an initial cash payment.

This change reflects updated estimates of ESSA’s cash balance at closing and potential liabilities. ESSA will file the amended agreement on a Current Report on Form 8-K.

XOMA Royalty (XOMA) remains the structuring agent and intends to finance the deal.

Source: Press Release

data.title: Trump: Ukraine, with EU support, can retake all its territory
data.description:
President Donald Trump has said Ukraine can take back all its territory lost to Russia,  and told the media that he believes NATO nations should shoot down Russian aircraft violating their airspace.

"After getting to know and fully understand the Ukraine/Russia Military and Economic situation and, after seeing the Economic trouble it is causing Russia, I think Ukraine, with the support of the European Union, is in a position to fight and WIN all of Ukraine back in its original form," Trump posted on Truth Social.

"With time, patience, and the financial support of Europe and, in particular, NATO, the original Borders from where this War started, is very much an option," he added.

Trump affirmed that the U.S. will continue to supply weapons to NATO for the alliance "to do what they want with them."

The sudden shift in Ukraine's favor comes after Trump previously suggested that Kyiv should consider giving up territory to bring an end to the war. Moscow controls about 20% of Ukrainian territory.

While addressing the UN General Assembly on Tuesday, Trump said the U.S. is prepared to impose "a very strong round of powerful tariffs" if Russia is not ready to make a deal to end the war. 

Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy lauded Trump's statements on Ukraine retaking territory, while the Kremlin brushed them off.

data.title: CareTrust REIT expands into UK with acquisition of two care homes for $27M
Mercedes-Benz names Jörg Burze to technology chief
data.description:
• Mercedes-Benz (OTCPK:MBGAF) (OTCPK:MBGYY) reshuffled its top management, Jörg Burze, currently head of production, has been appointed as the new CTO, Reuters reported.
  • He succeeds Markus Schaefer, who is leaving the company after a 30-year tenure.
  • Burzer will officially assume responsibility for development and procurement in his new role as CTO starting December 1, 2025.
  • Michael Schiebe, currently head of the high-performance Mercedes-AMG division, will move to the management board as the new head of production, quality, and supply chain management, succeeding Jörg Burze.
  • "With Joerg Burzer and Michael Schiebe, two outstanding managers from our own ranks are taking over key departments that are of decisive importance for the future success of the Mercedes-Benz Group." Mercedes supervisory board head Martin Brudermueller commented.
  • Schiebe is 42 years old and brings his Mercedes-AMG leadership experience to this new production chief role.

data.title: Chinese tech stocks surge on Alibaba AI spending boost
data.description:
Chinese tech stocks extended their rally on Wednesday, with Alibaba (NYSE:BABA) leading the charge.

Alibaba's Hong Kong-listed shares surged above 9% after the e-commerce giant announced plans to ramp up artificial intelligence spending past an original $50 billion-plus target and unveiled its largest-ever language model, the Qwen3-Max. The company also said it plans to integrate Nvidia's suite of AI development tools for 'Physical AI' into its cloud software platform.

The Hang Seng Tech Index, which tracks the largest tech firms listed in Hong Kong, also rose 2.7% and has soared 46% year-to-date, driven in part by optimism around AI investments and substantial fundraising by major companies.

Alibaba’s NYSE-listed ADRs also climbed 9.2%, while U.S.-listed shares of other major Chinese tech companies jumped in premarket trading: Baidu (BIDU) +4.6%, JD.com (JD) +3.7%, Bilibili (BILI) +3.2%, KE Holdings (BEKE) +2.4%, Baozun (BZUN) +1.9%, and Trip.com Group (TCOM) +0.7%. Other Chinese tech majors to watch: NetEase (NTES) and Tencent (OTCPK:TCEHY).

data.title: TotalEnergies, RWE to build $5.3B French wind farm after winning tender
data.description:
• A consortium of oil and gas major TotalEnergies (NYSE:TTE) and RWE has been chosen to develop the Centre Manche 2 offshore wind project by France's Ministry of Industry and Energy, according to a statement on Wednesday.
  • The project will represent an overall investment of approximately €4.5 billion, the largest made by TotalEnergies (NYSE:TTE) in the country in the past 30 years.
  • Located more than 40 km off the coast of Normandy, this will be the largest renewable energy project ever developed in France.
  • Once built, it will generate approximately 6 TWh per year and supply green electricity to the equivalent of over 1 million French households. The electricity will be sold at a competitive price of €66/MWh, as set by the tender.
  • TotalEnergies will be the operator of the project and will continue the necessary studies to reach a final investment decision by early 2029.
